markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Embed Monaco in code block nodes for language-aware editing without launching an external editor. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: MonacoEmbeddedCodeBlock leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoEmbeddedCodeBlock is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified bc476ece: monaco_code_block_node.test.ts 5/5 + MonacoCodeBlockView.test.tsx 2/2 - embedded Monaco code-block node. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: H4 stale-closure language in Monaco onDidChangeContent (hash minted against mount-time language after a switch)
evidence.fix: languageRef feeds the persistent change listener the CURRENT language; all attr writes (Monaco listener, language picker, degraded textarea) mint through makeCodeBlockAttrs
tests: MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx: hashes keystrokes against the CURRENT language after a language switch (RED on pre-fix code via stash-run: ts-hash 38e53d7c vs expected json-hash 5f6dfc9b)
MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx: hashes keystrokes against the mount language when never switched
MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx: language switch alone re-mints the hash for the existing code

evidence.finding: M6 keyboard trap: no keyboard path out of the embedded Monaco editor (WCAG 2.1.2)
evidence.fix: Escape exits to prose with caret placed after the block: Monaco addCommand keybinding context-guarded for find/suggest/rename popups + identical exit on the degraded textarea; aria-label + data-keyboard-exit document the affordance
tests: RichTextEditor.a11y.test.tsx: exits the embedded code block to the prose document with Escape (degraded path)
rich_editor_typing.spec.ts: M6/M17 (browser) Escape exits the embedded Monaco editor back to prose typing (13/13 lane green)

evidence.finding: M5 setValue destroys Monaco undo/caret/view state + M7 dual undo stacks diverge
evidence.fix: external reconciles apply via model.pushEditOperations wrapped in saveViewState/restoreViewState; M7 undo-ownership contract documented at the reconcile site (PM owns document-level history, Monaco owns intra-block text history; PM undo flows through the reconcile as a Monaco edit)
tests: MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx: reconciles EXTERNAL code changes via pushEditOperations with view-state preserve, never setValue
rich_editor_typing.spec.ts: EXT-UNDO smoke (Mod-z reverts typed text in the browser)

evidence.finding: L7 no lazy mount (every block boots Monaco at parse) + L6 disposition
evidence.fix: IntersectionObserver-gated mount (600px rootMargin; immediate without IO; find-reveal force-mounts and replays); static code preview while unmounted (never blank). L6 deferred with design rationale (attr writes are the persistence bridge).
tests: MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx: mounts Monaco lazily on viewport intersection, showing a code preview meanwhile
playwright dependency lane 13/13 green against rebuilt harness (real IO mounts)

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: monaco_code_block_node.test.ts 5/5 + MonacoCodeBlockView.test.tsx PASS; playwright offline mount PASS (data-monaco-mounted=true)

findings.title: Stale-closure language in onDidChangeContent corrupts the round-trip hash after a language switch
findings.evidence: MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:52-95 mounts once and captures `language` from the FIRST render inside the model.onDidChangeContent closure (line 73: codeBlockHash(language, next)). After the operator changes the language picker (updateAttributes language+hash, lines 121-126), every subsequent keystroke recomputes contentHash with the OLD language -> persisted attrs where contentHash != codeBlockHash(attrs.language, attrs.code). The MT-168 integrity invariant is silently broken at the source.
findings.fix: Keep a languageRef updated by the language effect (or read the current node attrs at write time) so the hash is always computed from the current language; add a test: mount -> change language -> type -> verifyCodeBlockIntegrity(attrs) === true.

findings.title: External reconcile uses model.setValue -> Monaco undo stack, caret, scroll and folds destroyed on every external code change
findings.evidence: MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:99-107 - any externally-driven attrs.code change (prose-side undo/redo of code edits, future collab updates, document reload) runs instance.setValue(code), which resets the model: undo history cleared, cursor to 0:0, view state lost.
findings.fix: Replace setValue with model.pushEditOperations(minimal diff) and save/restore editor view state around external updates (the standard Monaco reconcile pattern).

findings.title: Keyboard trap: no keyboard path out of the embedded Monaco editor
findings.evidence: monaco_code_block_node.ts is an atom node with no addKeyboardShortcuts; MonacoCodeBlockView wires no boundary handling. Once focus is inside Monaco, Tab indents (Monaco), arrows never exit at first/last line, and Escape does nothing -> a keyboard-only user cannot leave the code block (WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap).
findings.fix: Implement the standard code-block NodeView escape pattern: ArrowDown on last line / ArrowUp on first line moves selection to the adjacent prose block (creating a trailing paragraph if the block is last); Escape selects the node; consider Monaco tabFocusMode toggle.

findings.title: Dual undo stacks (Monaco internal vs ProseMirror attr history) diverge across the boundary
findings.evidence: Every Monaco keystroke emits a PM attr-update transaction (updateAttributes, MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:73) entering StarterKit history; Monaco keeps its own model undo. Prose-side Ctrl+Z undoes an attr step -> effect setValue resets the Monaco stack; Monaco-side Ctrl+Z afterwards mutates code and creates NEW PM steps. Mixed sequences produce surprising states; no test covers cross-boundary undo.
findings.fix: Pick one owner: suppress PM history for code-attr transactions (setMeta addToHistory false + explicit commit points) or disable Monaco internal undo and route Ctrl+Z to PM. Document the chosen semantics and test prose-edit -> code-edit -> undo -> undo.

findings.title: Code text serialized twice in HTML rendering and full code re-pushed through attrs each keystroke
findings.evidence: monaco_code_block_node.ts:95-108 renderHTML emits a data-code attribute AND the code text inside <code> (2x payload for copy/export); every keystroke ships the FULL code string through updateAttributes -> editor.getJSON() upstream (O(code length) per keystroke).
findings.fix: Drop data-code and parse from <code> textContent (parseAttrsFromElement already falls back to it); consider debouncing attr writes (e.g. 150ms idle) with flush-on-blur/save.
findings.residual: deliberate design: the <pre data-code>+<code> double serialization is the round-trip safety net for non-NodeView renders, and per-keystroke attr writes ARE the MT-168 single-authority-record persistence bridge; debouncing them risks losing trailing keystrokes on an immediate save. Revisit only with a flush-on-save contract (workbench shell lane MT-245+).

findings.title: No lazy mount: every code block boots a full Monaco instance at parse time
findings.evidence: Each monacoCodeBlock NodeView constructs an IStandaloneCodeEditor immediately; a document with dozens of code blocks mounts dozens of Monaco instances up front (memory + startup cost).
findings.fix: Mount Monaco on first interaction or via IntersectionObserver; render the serialized <pre><code> until visible.

parity_gaps: ED-CODE-INT-006 folding/bracket matching: Monaco defaults active inside the embed but unconfigured/unverified (no test)
ED-CODE-INT-009 minimap explicitly disabled, sticky scroll/inlay hints unconfigured (accepted as P2)
Monaco theme hardcoded vs-dark; no app-theme sync (ED-WB-009 adjacency)
missing_tests: language change then edit keeps verifyCodeBlockIntegrity true
external attrs.code update preserves Monaco caret/undo (after pushEditOperations fix)
keyboard-only enter/exit of the code block (boundary arrows + Escape)
cross-boundary undo sequence (prose edit -> code edit -> Ctrl+Z x2)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-165 requires embedding a real bundled Monaco editor inside a Tiptap code-block node for language-aware in-document editing without an external editor. The code delivers a real monacoCodeBlock atom node + React NodeView that mounts the lockfile-governed, offline-bundled Monaco (no CDN), is language-aware over the curated registry, bridges code+language+round-trip hash into node attrs, degrades gracefully to an editable textarea, and is proven by both vitest and a real-browser offline Playwright suite. All prior adversarial-review HIGH/MEDIUM findings (H4 stale-closure hash, M5 setValue, M6 keyboard trap, M7 undo) are fixed in current code with backing tests.
findings: app/src/lib/tiptap/monaco_code_block_node.ts:57-157 — real Tiptap atom Node 'monacoCodeBlock' (matching MT-161 inventory): addAttributes for language/code/contentHash, parseHTML/renderHTML round-trip through <pre data-*><code> (priority 100 to outrank StarterKit's generic pre), renderText emits a fenced block for markdown projection, addCommands(insertMonacoCodeBlock/setMonacoCodeBlockLanguage) mint through makeCodeBlockAttrs (MT-168 single-minting-point). No placeholders.

app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:192-283 — mounts a REAL bundled Monaco via createConfiguredEditor (the single product entry point), language-aware, writes attrs on every model change reading the CURRENT language via languageRef (H4 fix, lines 77-80,229-237), graceful degrade to an editable textarea on mount failure with a typed dependency-failure report (lines 262-273, never blank/lost). M6 keyboard-trap exit on Escape (lines 89-99,241-245) context-guarded for Monaco popups.

app/src/lib/monaco/setup.ts:1-67,134-166 — Monaco is the lockfile npm package with Vite-bundled `?worker` chunks (same-origin, offline, CSP-safe); the CDN @monaco-editor/loader is explicitly forbidden and not imported. createConfiguredEditor is the single mount entry point routing worker/failure policy through the typed dependency-failure surface. Satisfies the packet 'no CDN editor workers' forbiddance and MT 'no external network' constraint.

tests/dependency_policy/rich_editor_typing.spec.ts:160-499 — REAL-browser proof against the BUILT dist-harness over a loopback static server with ALL non-loopback requests aborted and asserted empty (lines 178-195). It mounts real Monaco (data-monaco-mounted=true), types into Monaco, switches language ts->js, independently recomputes the FNV round-trip hash (lines 290-327, H4 browser proof), and proves Escape exits the code island back to prose (lines 483-499, M6/M17). This is the offline/bundled-worker runtime touch of the real Handshake-native implementation, not a mock.

tests/dependency_policy/offline_editor_load.spec.ts:1-19 — proves Monaco mounts from bundled assets with a REAL TypeScript-worker round-trip while the network is cut and the request ledger is empty, confirming the bundled-worker boot rather than a CDN.

app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.language.test.tsx + monaco_code_block_node.test.ts — vitest schema/round-trip/integrity coverage: insert mints normalized language + verifyCodeBlockIntegrity hash, ProseMirror JSON load->reload round-trip, language switch keeps the hash, H4 current-language hashing, M5 pushEditOperations-not-setValue with view-state preserve, L7 lazy IntersectionObserver mount with a non-blank preview.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): the MT-165 lifecycle.evidence cites a follow-on `gov(WP-KERNEL-005) MT-165,170,188 V2 (proven on live PG)` commit (ca38ded7) and v02.192 spec anchors; spec/governance pointer drift is expected during repo-governance winddown and is not a code defect. The MT-165 code deliverable itself is a UI/editor surface whose durable persistence is content_json via the rich-doc save API owned by sibling MTs, so no MT-165-local PostgreSQL row is required.
evidence_checked: git show --stat bc476ece + git log --grep MT-165 (commits bc476ece, ca38ded7)
grep for CDN/sqlite/external-search/deferred-live escapes across app/src monaco surface (no matches)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
